title: brickellia fendleri var. typica
intro: Brickellia fendleri var. typica, also known as Fendler's brickellia or Fendler's mountain sage, is a plant species in the family Asteraceae. It is a shrubby perennial herb native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, valued for its medicinal properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions due to its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of fever, respiratory issues, and skin infections. The leaves and flowers of the plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal benefits and promote overall well-being.
